mnemonics: Low-Power PANs: 802.15.4 is For (Low) Powering Personal Area Networks. Think of 15.4 as the number of watts it uses, and 4 as the number of devices in a tiny network.
keyPoints: IEEE 802.15.4 defines the physical (PHY) and media access control (MAC) layer standards for low-data-rate, low-power wireless personal area networks (LR-WPANs).
It is the foundation for many high-level IoT protocols and standards, including Zigbee, 6LoWPAN, and Thread.
It operates in various frequency bands including 2.4 GHz (global), 915 MHz (Americas), and 868 MHz (Europe), with data rates from 20 kbps to 250 kbps.
commonMistakes: Confusing IEEE 802.15.4 (the low-level standard) with Zigbee (a high-level protocol that builds on it).
Assuming it is designed for long-range communication; it is actually optimized for short-range, low-power applications.
